name: rag_web_browser
title: RAG Web Browser
description: Fetch one public web page through a browser and return clean RAG-ready text, Markdown, headings, links, images, JSON-LD type hints, and query snippets.

seo.title: RAG Web Browser Tool for AI Agents
seo.description: Render a public URL with Better Fetch and return clean text, Markdown, headings, links, images, JSON-LD type hints, and query snippets for RAG and AI agent workflows.
seo.intro: RAG Web Browser gives agents a focused retrieval primitive: open one public web page in a real browser, remove noisy markup, and return clean content that is easy to cite, embed, summarize, or route into a retrieval pipeline. It preserves the source URL, canonical URL, title, headings, Markdown-style body text, link citations, and optional query snippets so downstream LLM steps can reason over the page without parsing HTML.

useCases.title: RAG ingestion
useCases.description: Fetch a URL and convert it into normalized text and Markdown before saving it into a vector store or knowledge base.

useCases.title: Agent browsing
useCases.description: Let an agent read a page, inspect headings, follow citation links, and decide what to fetch next.

useCases.title: Source-grounded summaries
useCases.description: Return page text plus title, canonical URL, links, and query snippets so summaries can stay grounded in the source material.

faqs.question: Does RAG Web Browser search the web?
faqs.answer: This v0.1 fetches one URL at a time. Google search orchestration is intentionally left for a later version so the published tool stays predictable and easy to validate.

faqs.question: What is the difference between text and markdown?
faqs.answer: text is the cleaned body content joined as plain text. markdown keeps simple heading, list, and quote structure so an LLM can preserve document shape.

faqs.question: Can it fetch dynamic pages?
faqs.answer: Yes. It renders through the Better Fetch browser engine with a configurable wait time, then extracts the visible HTML returned by that browser session.

url.description: Public page URL to render and extract

query.description: Optional query terms used to return simple match counts and snippets from the extracted text

max_text_chars.description: Maximum characters to return in text and markdown

max_links.description: Maximum page links to return as citation candidates

wait_ms.description: Additional browser wait time after DOMContentLoaded

country.description: Optional two-letter country code for geo-localized public pages

markdown.description: Markdown-style content preserving headings, lists, and quotes

reading_time_minutes.description: Approximate reading time at 225 words per minute

json_ld_types.description: JSON-LD @type values found on the page

count.description: Simple substring count in extracted text

snippets.description: Short snippets around matched query terms
